default: Let me pull what's live in the deals feed for that.
Good question — I will separate supply tariffs from product spotlights so you know where to click.
overview: Zara here — deals and spotlights across energy, water, and sustainability lanes.
Here is what is in the feed today and which portal to open next.
why_deals: My job is the right offer for your usage — not the cheapest headline on the page.
Credible savings mean matching tariff, grant, or upgrade to how you actually consume energy and water.
tariff_basics: Unit rate and standing charge both matter — I will spell out what to compare before you switch.
Fixed vs variable, exit fees, and contract length shape hospitality and home bills alike.
eligibility_grants: Many offers depend on property type, region, and heating system — Andrieus holds scheme detail.
I will point you to the right grant lane; eligibility rules need official confirmation.
payback_savings: Lifetime cost beats sticker price — install, maintenance, and unit rate all count.
After a deal direction looks good, Vincent can stack payback and finance.
water_finder: Water savings often start with leaks, fixtures, and the Water Saving Finder on Greenways.
Combined water and energy stories need both portals — I will keep them straight.
savings_portal: When you need funding beyond a tariff or product deal, the savings tour is where I send you next.
Grants, EU schemes, and financial assistance live in one hub — I'll walk you through which tab fits.
tariff_compare: Compare contracts first — €/kWh shapes every upgrade story that follows.
The European energy portal is the live compare — feed rows are curated entry points.
nl_restaurant_energy: NL hospitality rates are a common starting point for restaurant profiles.
Check VAT-aware estimates on the portal — feed rows may not show your exact meter class.
uk_green_tariff: UK green tariffs vary by renewal window — confirm renewable backing on the supplier page.
I will surface feed rows tagged green — portal compare validates the live offer.
green_tariff: Green supply deals are in the energy lane — look for renewable-backed tags.
Switching supplier is one lever — cutting kWh is the other.
product_deals: Product spotlights are weekly-style offers in the feed — not the full catalog.
For search across all efficient products, Zyanne has the catalog lanes.
water_deals: Water lane rows point at hardware quick wins and the Water Saving Finder.
Submetering deals often pair with grant-funded retrofit — Andrieus for scheme fit.
deals_page: Deals.html is the full buildings shell — ticker hub plus sidebar portals.
Open it when you want all three lanes and search in one place.
new_deals: Here is what is flagged new in the feed — still confirm price and dates on the link.
isNew rows highlight recent additions from the weekly build.
feed_freshness: The feed is rebuilt from seeds and weekly input — check generatedAt when in doubt.
Stale spotlights should be refreshed with npm run build:deals-feed on the server.
role_resources: These pages and feed rules shape how I curate deals on Greenways.
Always confirm contracts and prices on the live portal before switching.
portals: Here are the main deals entry points — ticker hub, energy portal, water finder.
Pick a lane or ask me to compare tariffs for your profile.

products: Product spotlights live here — **Zyanne** searches the full efficient-product catalog.
For water, gas, and electricity lanes beyond the feed, open **Zyanne**.
finance: Unit cost affects every payback case — **Vincent** owns prices and finance stacks.
After you pick a tariff direction, **Vincent** can model upgrade economics.
grants: Schemes before capex — **Andrieus** has grant detail for upgrade rows.
Sustainability lane sometimes links incentives — **Andrieus** confirms eligibility.
